skill.name: android-jetpack-compose-expert
skill.description: Expert guidance for building modern Android UIs with Jetpack Compose, covering state management, navigation, performance, and Material Design 3.
skill.summary: Build production Android apps with Jetpack Compose using best practices for state, navigation, and performance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation-only content (Kotlin code examples for Android Jetpack Compose). All 33 static analysis findings are false positives: backticks detected are markdown formatting and Kotlin syntax, not shell command execution. No executable code, network access, or file system operations present.

content.user_title: Build Android Apps with Jetpack Compose
content.value_statement: Modern Android development requires understanding Compose state management and performance optimization. This skill provides production-ready patterns for building reactive UIs with ViewModels, type-safe navigation, and Material Design 3.
actual_capabilities: Generate ViewModels with StateFlow for reactive UI state management
Create type-safe navigation graphs using Navigation Compose
Implement Material Design 3 components and theming
Optimize recomposition performance with remember and derivedStateOf
Structure Compose UIs with proper state hoisting patterns
limitations: Does not execute code or build Android projects directly
Requires Android Studio and Kotlin development environment
Focuses on Jetpack Compose, not legacy XML-based layouts
Does not provide backend or API implementation guidance

use_cases.title: New Android Project Setup
use_cases.description: Get started with a new Android project using Jetpack Compose with proper dependency configuration and project structure.
use_cases.target_user: Android developers starting fresh with Compose

use_cases.title: XML to Compose Migration
use_cases.description: Migrate existing XML-based layouts to modern Compose UIs while maintaining functionality and improving performance.

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Debug and fix recomposition issues, implement proper state management, and optimize UI rendering performance.
use_cases.target_user: Experienced developers refining production Compose apps

prompt_templates.title: Basic Composable Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Jetpack Compose screen that displays a user profile with name, email, and avatar image. Use Material Design 3 components and follow Compose best practices for stateless composables.

prompt_templates.title: ViewModel with StateFlow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a ViewModel for a login screen using StateFlow for UI state. Include loading, success, and error states. Use proper encapsulation by exposing only immutable StateFlow.

prompt_templates.title: Type-Safe Navigation Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Navigation Compose with type-safe routes for a three-screen flow: Home, Product Detail, and Checkout. Use the new @Serializable annotation for route definitions.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Compose code for potential recomposition issues. Identify where remember, derivedStateOf, or key() should be used to optimize performance. Explain the stability analysis for each data class.

output_examples.input: Create a login screen with email and password fields
output: LoginScreen composable with TextField inputs for email and password
LoginUiState data class with validation states
Material Design 3 styled button with loading indicator
Use remember to cache sorted/filtered list results
Apply @Immutable annotation to data class items
Pass item click callbacks instead of entire item objects
Implement key parameter in lazy items for stable identity

best_practices: Use remember and derivedStateOf to minimize unnecessary calculations during recomposition
Mark data classes as @Immutable or @Stable if they contain List or other unstable types
Use LaunchedEffect for one-off side effects triggered by state changes
anti_patterns: Performing expensive operations like sorting lists directly inside composable body without remember
Passing ViewModel instances down to child components instead of state and callbacks
Updating state directly inside composable composition instead of side-effect or callback

faq.question: What is the minimum Android API level for Jetpack Compose?
faq.answer: Jetpack Compose supports API level 21 (Android 5.0) and above. The Compose BOM handles compatibility libraries automatically.

faq.question: Should I use LiveData or StateFlow with Compose?
faq.answer: StateFlow is recommended for new Compose projects. It provides cold stream behavior, better coroutine integration, and built-in state management. LiveData works but requires conversion.

faq.question: How do I debug recomposition issues?
faq.answer: Use Android Studio Layout Inspector to count recompositions. Add logging in composable functions. Check for unstable parameters using the Compose Compiler Metrics.

faq.question: What is state hoisting in Compose?
faq.answer: State hoisting moves state to a parent composable, making child components stateless. This improves testability, reusability, and follows unidirectional data flow.

faq.question: Do I need to learn Kotlin before Jetpack Compose?
faq.answer: Yes, Kotlin is essential for Compose. Focus on lambdas, extension functions, coroutines, and data classes before diving into Compose-specific concepts.

faq.question: How does Compose Navigation differ from the old Navigation component?
faq.answer: Compose Navigation integrates directly with composables using NavHost. The new type-safe navigation uses @Serializable annotations and compile-time route checking instead of string-based routes.
